finops.workspace.title: Costs, inventory and recommendations in a single view.
finops.workspace.description: FinOps is now presented as a unified workspace. From here you can analyze real spend, review multi-account inventory and run advanced analyses that relate cost and usage.
finops.workspace.tabCostsDesc: CUR and Athena to understand spend concentration, trends and relevant variations by service and account.
finops.workspace.tabInventoryDesc: Multi-account inventory with operational context to detect coverage, sprawl and governance debt.
finops.workspace.tabAdvisorDesc: Deep analysis crossing inventory, usage and real cost with evidence layer, dataset coverage and operational gaps.
finops.workspace.tabK8sDesc: Real cost per cluster, namespace and workload from OpenCost metrics in Grafana. Includes CPU/RAM efficiency, network egress, load balancers and rightsizing candidates.
finops.workspace.tabChatDesc: Ask in natural language about your AWS spend. The assistant queries CUR/Athena, Cost Explorer and the daily inventory for you.
finops.workspace.legacyNote: Previous access paths remain active and redirect to this unified view.

inventory.topServicesDesc: Visible ranking by AWS family. Replaces the donut for a much clearer reading.
inventory.noServicesMatch: No services match the current scope.
inventory.resourcesByAccountDesc: Visible totals per AWS account after applying filters and search.
inventory.noAccountsMatch: No visible accounts with the current scope.
eng.title: Engineering & Quality Metrics
eng.description: Consolidated view of DORA metrics, Merge Request flow and code quality. The DORA layer uses GitLab as the traceability base between commits, Merge Requests and deployments; CFR and Pipeline Recovery Time are only reinforced with runtime correlation when coverage is sufficient. Kubernetes provides operational validation and SonarQube is shown as an independent quality reading.
eng.filtersDesc: These filters govern DORA and MR analytics. SonarQube can automatically inherit mapped GitLab projects and always allows manual override.
advisor.title: FinOps AI Advisor
advisor.description: Intelligent analysis of inventory, CloudWatch metrics and CUR costs
advisor.pageDescription: Intelligent analysis crossing inventory, usage metrics and costs with AI, deterministic evidence and real dataset coverage.
advisor.includeCosts: Include real CUR/Athena costs in the report
advisor.fullModeNote: Full mode (metrics + CUR) takes longer, but improves report accuracy.
advisor.tagsForOwnership: useful tags for search and ownership
advisor.prioritizedOpportunitiesDesc: Deterministic findings calculated before the LLM. They serve as an objective basis for prioritizing the report.
advisor.unmatchedSpendDesc: Services with the most real cost without resource link. This is the best guide for prioritizing matching improvements.
inventory.serviceBreakdownDesc: Now clearly separates AWS family and resource type, and adds estimated cost and real Terraform status.
inventory.name: Name
inventory.accountBreakdownDesc: Visible detail per account, with services and aggregated estimated cost.
inventory.exportColumnsDesc: Select the columns you want to include in the Excel export of the visible scope.
eng.prodDeliveryDesc: Changes actually deployed to production, deduplicated by commit, to complement the MR reading.
eng.deliveryCoverageSub: Changes in prod vs merged MRs
eng.weeklyProdImpactDesc: Evolution of deployments, actual production changes and weekly median lead time.
eng.teamsInProdDesc: Ranking by actually deployed changes and associated corrective signal.
eng.noTraceability: There is not enough production traceability for this scope.
eng.authorFocusDesc: Summary of the filtered author and their most recent changes that reached production.
eng.weeklyRhythmDesc: Weekly volume with review time and lead time medians to identify bottlenecks.
eng.reviewDistributionDesc: Mean, median, dispersion and outlier reading on the review flow.
eng.collaboratorRankingDesc: Sorted by reviews performed and actual collaboration scope within the period.
eng.explainTotalLines: Total lines added and removed by contributors in the selected period, based on commit data from developer_activity_daily.
eng.bottlenecksDesc: Merge Requests with the longest review time within the filtered period.
eng.noBottlenecks: Not enough merged Merge Requests to detect bottlenecks.
eng.authorsInProdTableDesc: People whose changes have reached production, with scope, lead time and risk signal reading.
eng.noAuthorsInProd: No deployed changes with resolved author for this scope yet.
eng.recentProdChangesDesc: Operational drill-down to connect author, commit, deployment and observed lead time.
eng.author: Author
eng.noRecentProdChanges: No recent production changes for this scope.
eng.recentMRsDesc: Latest MRs of the period with state, size and review times.
eng.noMRs: No Merge Requests for the current scope.
eng.coverageAndDuplicationDesc: Historical trend of average coverage and duplication density over the selected portfolio.
eng.aggregatedRiskDesc: Combined reading of bugs, vulnerabilities and hotspots to prioritize actions on the visible portfolio.
eng.highestRiskDesc: Heuristic index: vulnerabilities x4, bugs x2, hotspots x1.5, gate ERROR +20 and penalty for coverage <80%.
eng.lowestCoverageDesc: Projects below the rest in coverage
eng.mostDebtDesc: Projects where the remediation effort seems highest.
eng.sonarPortfolioDesc: Only Sonar projects currently selected in the effective scope are shown.
eng.exportSonarPortfolioDesc: The Excel will only include the Sonar projects currently visible in this table. Choose the columns you want to include in the report.
eng.selectAtLeastOneColumn: Select at least one column for the export.
eng.exportError: Could not generate the Sonar portfolio Excel.
eng.noHistoricalData: Not enough historical data to build this report.
eng.sonarScopeEmpty: The Sonar scope is empty for the selected GitLab projects.
eng.selectProjectForMetrics: Select at least one project to view metrics
inventory.exportError: Could not export the Excel file.
data.label: Data
costs.top10servicesDesc: Accumulated spend ranking by service within the filtered scope.
costs.top10accountsDesc: Accumulated spend ranking by account within the filtered scope.
costs.noDataInScope: No data in the current scope.
costs.periodComparisonDesc: Total cost of the filtered scope versus the previous comparable period.
costs.monthlyMultiDesc: Consolidated total line and one series per account to quickly see who drives the trend.
costs.monthlySingleDesc: Consolidated monthly spend series for the selected account to provide context for the current comparison.
costs.noMonthlyData: No monthly history available for the selected scope.
costs.noResourceBreakdown: No resource breakdown in the CUR for this service
costs.noAccountsMatch: No accounts match the current scope.
costs.exportFailed: Failed to export Excel file. Please try again.
exec.selectProjectForMetrics: Select at least one project to view metrics
eng.pipelineRecoveryTimeDesc: Measures the time between a failed pipeline and the next successful pipeline in the same project. Not equivalent to standard DORA MTTR, which requires correlation with real incidents.
eng.uniqueCommitsTooltip: Distinct commits observed in production within the current period, deduplicated across the entire scope and not just per day.
eng.authorsFilteredTooltip: Authors currently selected in the management tab.
eng.explainDeployFreq: Cadence of successful production deployments based on the portal's GitLab canonical layer. The value is normalized per active project and day within the visible scope.
eng.explainLeadTime: Time from the first traceable commit of the change to production. Prefers the first commit as the base; if that reference is missing, falls back to MR creation and, ultimately, to the last traced commit.
eng.explainCfrGitlab: Operational proxy for change failure rate in GitLab: deployment job failures divided by total attempts. Hotfixes and rollbacks are classified but do not increment this metric on their own.
eng.explainMttrGitlab: Operational Pipeline Recovery Time: time between a failed job or pipeline and the next successful event in the same scope observed in GitLab. Not equivalent to standard DORA MTTR.
eng.explainThroughput: Total number of Merge Requests merged in the selected period. Each MR is counted only once.
eng.explainOpenMRs: Merge Requests currently in open state within the selected scope. Indicates the team's actual WIP.
eng.explainReviewDensity: Average number of review comments per MR. Measures the depth of the code review process.
eng.explainLifetime: Median total time from MR creation to merge. Only considers merged MRs.
eng.explainLeadTimeToFeedback: Median time from MR creation to the first review comment. Measures the team's response speed.
eng.explainReviewTime: Median active review time (from first comment to merge). Excludes initial wait time.
eng.explainChangeSize: Median change size on merged MRs, using aggregated additions + deletions.
eng.explainAging: Merge Requests still open whose current lifetime exceeds 3, 7 or 14 days. Useful for detecting stuck WIP and long review queues.
eng.explainChangesDeployed: Distinct commits that have reached production in the filtered period. A change is counted only once even if there are subsequent redeploys.
eng.explainAuthorsInProd: Unique authors with at least one deduplicated change that has reached production within the selected period and scope.
eng.explainLeadTimeToProd: Median lead time from commit to the first observed productive deployment for that change.
eng.explainHotfixes: Number of hotfix-type deployments touched by changes within the current scope.
eng.explainRollbacks: Number of deployments classified as rollback within the current scope.
eng.explainDeliveryCoverage: Approximate ratio between changes actually observed in production and the throughput of merged MRs in the same period.
eng.explainAvgCoverage: Average test coverage across all selected projects.
eng.explainVulnerabilities: Sum of security vulnerabilities detected by SonarQube in the selected projects.
eng.explainTechDebt: Estimated time needed to resolve all accumulated technical debt.
eng.mrExplorerDesc: Operational table to read agility detail per MR within the current scope.
eng.traceAvailable: The visible sample relies on {deploys} traced deployments, with {withMr} deployments linked to a Merge Request.
eng.traceNotAvailable: Not enough recent trace to show deployment detail.
eng.cfrMttrFromGitlab: CFR and Pipeline Recovery Time are read from GitLab
eng.withRuntimeReinforcement: with runtime reinforcement when correlation is sufficient ({pct}% coverage).
eng.sonarScopeEmptyHint: Sonar scope empty in the current selection
eng.allUpToDate: DORA, MR analytics and SonarQube are up to date within the current scope.
eng.sonarStaleMessage: DORA and MR are within the window, but SonarQube is outside the current one.
eng.autoSelectedSonar: Auto-selected {count} Sonar project(s) from the general GitLab filter, using persisted mapping or exact name convention.
eng.noSonarMapped: No Sonar project mapped for the current GitLab selection; the selector is free for manual choice.
eng.noGitlabSelected: No GitLab project selected: Sonar does not preselect any project and you can choose whichever you want.
eng.manualSonarMode: Manual selection active in SonarQube. You can return to automatic mapping whenever you want.
eng.noBottlenecksMsg: Not enough merged Merge Requests to detect bottlenecks.
eng.noTraceabilityMsg: Not enough production traceability for this scope.
eng.noRecentProdChangesMsg: No recent production changes for this scope.
eng.noMRsMsg: No Merge Requests for the current scope.
eng.sonarScopeEmptyAutoMsg: No auto-applied Sonar projects for the current GitLab scope. Manually choose one or more to see the exact portfolio here and export it to Excel.
eng.sonarSelectManualMsg: Select one or more Sonar projects to see only that exact portfolio here and be able to export it to Excel.
eng.sonarNoSnapshotMsg: The selected Sonar projects have no snapshot available within the current window.
eng.noHistoricalDataMsg: Not enough historical data to build this report.
eng.sonarScopeEmptyInfo: The Sonar scope is empty for the selected GitLab projects. If a mapping exists, it is auto-applied; otherwise, the selector is free for manual choice.
bench.prt.note: Approximate comparison: this metric measures pipeline recovery, not standard DORA MTTR.
eng.scopeTeamsTooltip: Number of teams visible in the current dashboard scope.
eng.scopeProjectsTooltip: GitLab repositories explicitly filtered in the general selector.
eng.sonarMappedTooltip: Sonar projects currently linked to GitLab.
eng.sonarUnmappedTooltip: Sonar projects not yet linked to GitLab.
eng.sonarMappingCoverageTooltip: Percentage of the available Sonar catalog already linked to GitLab.
eng.gitlabNoSonarTooltip: GitLab repositories selected in the general filter that do not yet have a mapped Sonar project.
eng.gitlabInFocusTooltip: GitLab repositories currently selected in the general filter.
eng.sonarAutoDetectedTooltip: Sonar projects automatically inherited from the general GitLab scope.
eng.effectiveSelectionTooltip: Number of Sonar projects that actually enter the current calculation.
eng.mrFilterNote: The tab follows the global team, project and author scope. The state selector only segments the MR explorer below.
eng.explainCfrHybrid: Hybrid rate: starts from deploy failures in GitLab and adjusts with correlated runtime failures (Argo/K8s) when coverage reaches {pct}%.
eng.explainMttrHybrid: Hybrid Pipeline Recovery Time: blends observed recovery in GitLab and correlated runtime with average confidence of {pct}%.

auto.cyber.vpnGroupsDesc: Lists the members of the AZ_VPN groups in Azure AD.

jira.description: Cycle time, workload distribution, open ticket aging and monthly trend. Connected to Jira Cloud in real time.
jira.selectProjectsHint: Select one or more Jira projects to view metrics.
jira.monthlyTrendDesc: Tickets created vs resolved per month within the selected window.
jira.assigneeRankingDesc: Assignee ranking by ticket volume in the window.
jira.serviceQueuesDesc: Service Desk queues for the selected projects.
advisor.aiReportDesc: AI-generated report based on collected inventory data, metrics and costs. Recommendations are based on the previous deterministic findings and the full analysis context.

infra.form.approverPlaceholder: Select who will approve this request

infra.form.teamDesc: Maps to the underlying Terraform repository (e.g. {repo}).
infra.form.environmentsDesc: Select the environments where this resource should be created.
infra.form.bucketNameDesc: Unique name for the S3 bucket.
infra.form.permissionsDesc: Select standard policies to attach to this role.
infra.form.irsaNote: IRSA Role: This will create an IAM Role trustable by a Kubernetes Service Account in the specified namespace.
infra.form.successMessage: Request submitted. A manager will review it and you will receive a notification when approved.
eng.insufficientCoverage: Insufficient data coverage: only {n} of {m} days have data
infraV2.validation.invalidIdentifier: Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens only. Between 3 and 63 characters, starts with a letter.
infraV2.validation.invalidDbName: Lowercase letters, numbers and underscores only. Between 1 and 63 characters, starts with a letter.
infraV2.validation.invalidBucketName: Lowercase letters, numbers, dots and hyphens only. Between 3 and 63 characters.
infraV2.validation.invalidRoleName: Alphanumeric, hyphens and underscores. Between 3 and 64 characters, starts with a letter.
infraV2.validation.invalidNamespace: Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens only. Between 1 and 63 characters, starts with a letter.
infraV2.status.error: Error generating Terraform. Please try again.
exec.lowConfidenceWarning: Low confidence: the displayed data may not be representative. Review the audit panel for details.
exec.lowCorrelationWarning: More than 30% of correlations have low confidence. The Change Failure Rate may be imprecise.
exec.leadTimeCanonicalDef: Canonical: time from first commit to production deploy
bench.leadTime.subtitle: from first commit to production deploy
bench.doraReference: Reference benchmarks from the Accelerate / DORA State of DevOps Report.
exec.pipelineRecoveryTime.tooltip: Measures the time between a failed pipeline and the next successful pipeline in the same scope. Not equivalent to standard DORA MTTR based on real incidents.
team.title: Team Activity
team.selectTeamPrompt: Select a team or project in the base filters to see individual activity.
team.selectTeamHint: Filters from the DORA tab are shared with this view.
request.noApprovalNeeded: This request type does not require approval

kiroAnalytics.subtitle: Kiro IDE usage analytics: adoption, classified prompts and activity.
kiroAnalytics.empty: No data for the selected filters
kiroAnalytics.kpi.wau.desc: Unique users who used Kiro in the last 7 days.

kiroAnalytics.kpi.chat.desc: Total messages in chat conversations with Kiro.
kiroAnalytics.kpi.hours.desc: Estimated time saved thanks to AI assistance.
kiroAnalytics.chart.featureAdoption.desc: Unique users who used each Kiro feature.
kiroAnalytics.chart.workType.desc: bug, feature, refactor, ktlo, testing, config, plan.
kiroAnalytics.chart.intent.desc: ask, write, plan, review, automate, explain.
kiroAnalytics.userActivity.notEnabled: User activity logging is not enabled in the Kiro Enterprise profile. Prompt data is available in the AI Insights tab.
kiroAnalytics.license.title: License usage
kiroAnalytics.license.subtitle: Per-user consumption: plan, clients, messages and credits (all accounts).
metrics.dora.scope.title: Scope
metrics.dora.deploymentLevel.label: Deployment/pipeline level
metrics.dora.deploymentLevel.tooltip: A failed deployment may mix changes from several authors. This metric is computed at the deployment/pipeline level and does not hold any individual person accountable.
metrics.dora.emptyState.title: No attributable activity in scope
metrics.dora.emptyState.description: There are no deployments or changes from these authors in the selected period, team and project. This is not an error: adjust the author filter or widen the scope.
metrics.dora.coverageNotice.warning: Best-effort author attribution: it may be incomplete.
metrics.dora.coverageNotice.note: Author attribution is based on the changes recorded in deployment_changes.
iam.arn.bad_format: The ARN is not in a valid format. Expected arn:aws:<service>:<region>:<account>:<resource>.
iam.arn.bad_account: The ARN account must be 12 digits.
iam.arn.cross_service: The ARN belongs to a different service than the selected permission.
iam.arn.wildcard_not_allowed: This permission does not allow wildcards in the ARN.
iam.arn.limit: The maximum limit of 50 ARNs per permission has been exceeded.
iam.catalog.unavailable: Permission options are not available right now. The request cannot proceed.
iam.validator.empty_or_malformed: The policy is empty or malformed; rejected for safety (default-deny).
iam.validator.managed_full_access: The managed policy grants full access (FullAccess); administrator policies are not allowed.
iam.validator.managed_administrator: The policy is an Administrator policy; administrator policies are not allowed.
iam.validator.wildcard_action_on_all_res: The policy grants wildcard actions on all resources; this is not allowed.
iam.validator.invalid_managed_arn: The managed policy ARN is not valid.
